last_excerpt: but it used to mean capable of sensation. A sensible shoe. A shoe that feels things. The sole as organ. --- Bread that's slightly stale is better for sandwiches. Everyone knows this and nobody says it. The specific embarrassment of getting something right for the wrong reasons. You guessed. You got lucky. The grade was good. The praise lands on a version of you that didn't do the thing they think you did. Hemlines. Whoever is tracking hemlines. The job of noticing a collective unconscious doing something with skirt lengths and writing it down. There's probably a spreadsheet somewhere. There's probably always a spreadsheet somewhere. The red squiggle under a word that's spelled correctly. The confidence of the squiggle. A level that's slightly off — you set it on the surface and the bubble doesn't quite center. Not off enough to matter. Off enough that you keep looking at it.
last_concepts: a window that won't stay open
a shadow that moves when you don't expect it
galvanized (zinc-coated steel — the particular silver-matte surface of it
door that pushes when it says pull
the way a cold window fogs from your breath
